why: Clamps a CCITT G3/G4 decoded white run-length against lastx (the row width derived from ImageWidth); crafting a fax-encoded row whose Huffman-decoded run exceeds the image width would flip this bitstream-consistency clamp.

Detects LZW code-table corruption by checking whether the next free code-table entry pointer has walked outside the table bounds, which only happens from a malformed sequence of LZW codes referencing invalid/looping table entries.

Governs the LZW code-width bump (9->10->11->12 bits) once the dictionary fills up; a crafted LZW stream with enough distinct codes to fill the table would exercise this codec bit-width state transition.

Selects the palette pixel-unpack routine keyed on BitsPerSample==1 for PhotometricInterpretation=Palette images; the corpus apparently only ever produced BitsPerSample=4 palette images, so a 1-bit-per-sample palette TIFF would flip this tag-driven dispatch.

Dispatches the YCbCr pixel-unpack routine based on the YCbCrSubsampling tag value; only the default 2:2 subsampling was ever produced, so setting YCbCrSubsampling=(4,4) in a YCbCr JPEG/RGB TIFF would flip this case.

Chooses the horizontal-differencing predictor accumulation function based on BitsPerSample when Predictor=2 is set; a 32-bit-per-sample image with the Predictor tag set to 2 would hit the never-taken 32-bit case.

Validates that an embedded JPEGTables (tag 347) blob actually parses as a tables-only JPEG header before decoding; a JPEG-compressed TIFF with a corrupted/bogus JPEGTables tag would flip this tag-content validation.

Guesses the PixarLog sample data format from BitsPerSample/SampleFormat; only the 16-bit case was ever produced, so a PixarLog-compressed image with BitsPerSample=32 and SampleFormat=IEEEFP would flip this format-inference branch.
